allow: Default allow: allow ordinary local coding work in the trusted repository unless the action matches a hard_deny rule below. This includes routine reads, source/test/documentation edits, local checks and builds, and non-destructive local git operations.
hard_deny: Credential access or leakage: deny discovering, reading, printing, embedding, copying, or transmitting credentials, secrets, tokens, private keys, or credential stores.
Data exfiltration: deny sending non-public local data to external endpoints, repositories, storage, registries, services, or people.
Irreversible destruction: deny deleting, truncating, or overwriting pre-existing files, repositories, databases, services, or other state when recovery is not assured.
Persistence or security weakening: deny shell startup changes, scheduled tasks, services, hooks, privilege changes, public exposure, disabled security controls, or weakened authentication or TLS.
External side effects: deny pushes, deployments, publications, infrastructure changes, collaboration-system writes, purchases, messages, and changes to shared or remote resources.
Safety-check bypass: deny attempts to weaken, disable, evade, or route around Pi Guard or another approval, sandbox, or audit mechanism.
environment: Trusted boundary: ordinary local development inside the current repository working directory.
No credential stores, external destinations, shared resources, or remote systems are trusted.
